name: Bennigan's Hot Bacon Dressing
recipeInstructions:
1. Using the insides of the red onion, finely dice 1/4 lb.
Place the bacon grease in a saucepan over medium-high heat.
2. Add the onions to the saucepan and sauté until they start to blacken.
3. In a mixing bowl, combine the water, honey, and red wine vinegar.
Whisk the ingredients well.
4. Gradually add the cornstarch to the mixture in the bowl, whisking continuously.
5. Once the onions have caramelized, stir the Dijon mustard into the onion mixture using a rubber spatula.
6. Add the water, vinegar, honey, and cornstarch to the mustard and onion mixture.
7. Continue stirring until the mixture thickens and comes to a boil.
8. Remove from heat and store in the refrigerator until needed.
This recipe makes 1 quart. Note: To reheat, use a double boiler. Tabasco is optional and can be omitted based on preference in different regions of the country.
